lines: // Lookup join after FORK is not support in CCS yet
+        // Lookup join after FROM-union subquery with remote indices is not supported in CCS yet
+        // Lookup join after INLINE STATS (coordinator-only) is not supported in CCS yet
+        // Lookup join after STATS (coordinator-only) is not supported in CCS yet
@@ -213,7 +220,8 @@ protected void shouldSkipTest(String testName) throws IOException {
"LOOKUP JOIN not yet supported in CCS",
@@ -245,11 +253,6 @@ protected void shouldSkipTest(String testName) throws IOException {
-            "skip CCS for IN subqueries until convertToRemoteIndices supports IN subquery",
-        // convertToRemoteIndices does not support WHERE IN subquery yet
-     * Convert index patterns and subqueries in FROM commands to use remote indices for a given test case.
+     * Convert index patterns and subqueries in FROM and WHERE IN subqueries to use remote
+     * indices for a given test case.
@@ -1702,7 +1702,13 @@ private static String unquote(String index, int numOfQuotes) {
-     * Convert index patterns and subqueries in FROM commands to use remote indices.
+     * Convert index patterns and subqueries in FROM and WHERE IN subqueries to use remote
+     * indices for a given test case.

+ *

Note: like {@link #splitIgnoringParentheses}, this method is not string-literal-aware.
+     * A literal {@code (}, {@code )}, or {@code |} inside a quoted string would be miscounted.
+     * The csv-spec test corpus contains no such literals in subquery tests, so this matches
// if there is metadata, we need to add it back later
-        // the main source command could be a comma separated list of index patterns, and subqueries
// Subqueries whose outer command is ROW (rather than FROM) still contain commas as part of ROW
// syntax — those must never be interpreted as UNION-of-sources branches nor rewritten into a FROM.
+        // The ROW source itself has no index to rewrite, but pipe segments that follow (e.g. WHERE IN)
+        // may contain FROM subqueries that do need rewriting.
// the main from command could be a comma separated list of index patterns, and subqueries
+        // Idempotency guard: if any plain (non-subquery) source already has been rewritten to a
+        // remote index pattern, skip conversion. This protects against double-rewriting when
+        // the same testcase instance is reused across @Repeat iterations.
// remove the FROM or TS keyword if it's there
-                // It's an index pattern, we need to convert it to remote index pattern.
// rebuild source command from transformed index patterns and subqueries, prepending any SET statements
String transformedFrom = setStatements + sourceCommand + " " + String.join(", ", transformed) + metadata;
+        // Rewrite any WHERE x IN (FROM ...) / NOT IN (...) subqueries in the pipeline segments
+        // that follow the source command. Non-subquery parenthesised groups (value lists, function
+        // arguments, boolean groupings) are left structurally unchanged.
+     * found inside an expression segment (e.g. a {@code WHERE} or {@code EVAL} clause) by
+     * recursively descending into every top-level parenthesised group.

+ *

Non-subquery parenthesised content — value lists such as {@code IN ("a","b")}, function
+     * arguments such as {@code COUNT(*)}, and boolean groupings such as
+     * {@code (a AND emp_no IN (FROM ...))} — is recursed to surface any nested subquery within,
+     * but the surrounding structure is otherwise left unchanged.
+                // find the matching close paren by scanning forward tracking depth
+                // segment[contentStart .. j-1] is the content inside the parens; j is past the ')'
+                    // This group is a subquery body — rewrite it recursively.
+                    // ROW bodies are returned unchanged by convertSubqueryToRemoteIndices.
+                    // Not a direct subquery body (value list, function args, boolean grouping, …).
+                    // Recurse into the raw content to catch any nested subquery inside it.
private static final Pattern FROM_COMMAND_PATTERN = commandPattern("from");
private static final Pattern TS_COMMAND_PATTERN = commandPattern("ts");
private static final Pattern ROW_COMMAND_PATTERN = commandPattern("row");
+        // The IN subquery body's FROM is rewritten; the outer FROM is also rewritten.
+                "FROM employees | WHERE emp_no IN (FROM employees | SORT emp_no ASC | LIMIT 3 | KEEP emp_no) | SORT emp_no | KEEP emp_no"
+                    + " | WHERE emp_no IN (FROM *:employees,employees | SORT emp_no ASC | LIMIT 3 | KEEP emp_no)"
+                "FROM employees | WHERE emp_no NOT IN (FROM employees | SORT emp_no ASC | LIMIT 3 | KEEP emp_no) | SORT emp_no"
+                    + " | WHERE emp_no NOT IN (FROM *:employees,employees | SORT emp_no ASC | LIMIT 3 | KEEP emp_no)"
+        // Literal value lists must NOT be rewritten — they don't contain a source command.
+        String query = "FROM employees | WHERE gender IN (\"F\", \"M\", null) | SORT emp_no";
+            equalTo("FROM *:employees,employees | WHERE gender IN (\"F\", \"M\", null) | SORT emp_no")
+        String query = "FROM employees | WHERE emp_no IN (10001, 10002, 10003) | SORT emp_no";
+            equalTo("FROM *:employees,employees | WHERE emp_no IN (10001, 10002, 10003) | SORT emp_no")
+        // Two levels of IN nesting: the inner IN body is inside the outer IN body.
+                    + " | WHERE salary IN (FROM employees | SORT salary DESC | LIMIT 3 | KEEP salary)"
+                    + " | WHERE salary IN (FROM *:employees,employees | SORT salary DESC | LIMIT 3 | KEEP salary)"
+        // FROM-union inside the IN subquery body: both union branches are rewritten.
+                    + " | WHERE emp_no IN (FROM (FROM employees | LIMIT 5), (FROM employees_incompatible | LIMIT 5) | KEEP emp_no)"
+        // ROW inside an IN subquery must be left untouched (no index to rewrite).
+            EsqlTestUtils.convertSubqueryToRemoteIndices("FROM employees | WHERE emp_no IN (ROW emp_no = 10001) | SORT emp_no"),
+            equalTo("FROM *:employees,employees | WHERE emp_no IN (ROW emp_no = 10001) | SORT emp_no")
+        // Two IN subqueries in a single WHERE clause — both must be rewritten.
+                    + " | WHERE emp_no NOT IN (FROM employees | LIMIT 3 | KEEP emp_no)"
+                    + " AND emp_no IN (FROM employees | LIMIT 10 | KEEP emp_no)"
+                    + " | WHERE emp_no NOT IN (FROM *:employees,employees | LIMIT 3 | KEEP emp_no)"
+                    + " AND emp_no IN (FROM *:employees,employees | LIMIT 10 | KEEP emp_no)"
+        // The IN subquery is nested inside a parenthesised boolean expression.
+                    + " | WHERE (emp_no NOT IN (FROM employees | LIMIT 5 | KEEP emp_no) AND salary > 70000)"
+                    + " OR emp_no IN (FROM employees | LIMIT 10 | KEEP emp_no)"
+                    + " | WHERE (emp_no NOT IN (FROM *:employees,employees | LIMIT 5 | KEEP emp_no) AND salary > 70000)"
+                    + " OR emp_no IN (FROM *:employees,employees | LIMIT 10 | KEEP emp_no)"
+                "ROW emp_no = 10007" + " | WHERE emp_no IN (FROM employees | WHERE salary > 70000 | KEEP emp_no)"
+            equalTo("ROW emp_no = 10007" + " | WHERE emp_no IN (FROM *:employees,employees | WHERE salary > 70000 | KEEP emp_no)")
+                "TS employees | WHERE emp_no IN (FROM employees | SORT emp_no ASC | LIMIT 3 | KEEP emp_no) | SORT emp_no | KEEP emp_no"
+                    + " | WHERE emp_no IN (FROM *:employees,employees | SORT emp_no ASC | LIMIT 3 | KEEP emp_no)"
+        // Multi-line formatting is handled: splitIgnoringParentheses joins the main pipe segments
+        // with " | ", collapsing newlines in the FROM clause. The subquery body inside the IN (...)
+        // parens is stripped of leading/trailing whitespace before recursion, so the surrounding
+        // newlines inside the parens are dropped (the rewritten subquery is structurally equivalent).
+                FROM employees | SORT emp_no ASC | LIMIT 3 | KEEP emp_no
+            + " | WHERE emp_no IN (FROM *:employees,employees | SORT emp_no ASC | LIMIT 3 | KEEP emp_no)"
+        // Calling the method twice on an already-converted query must return the query unchanged.
+            "FROM employees | WHERE emp_no IN (FROM employees | LIMIT 3 | KEEP emp_no) | SORT emp_no"
+     * Extends {@link #assertCCSExecutionInfoDetails} with exact shard-level checks for each cluster.
+     * Every cluster that appears in execution info must appear in {@code expectedTotalShardsPerCluster}
+     * and vice-versa; mismatches (unexpected cluster, missing cluster, wrong count) all cause assertion
+     * failures. Include the local cluster (key {@code ""}) when the query accesses local indices.
+            "mismatch in the number of expected clusters in the status output",
+            assertNotNull("cluster [" + clusterAlias + "] found in execution info but not in expected map", expected);
+            assertThat("cluster [" + clusterAlias + "] total shards", cluster.getTotalShards(), equalTo(expected));
+            assertThat("cluster [" + clusterAlias + "] all shards should have succeeded", cluster.getSuccessfulShards(), equalTo(expected));
+            assertNotNull("expected cluster [" + clusterAlias + "] is missing from execution info", executionInfo.getCluster(clusterAlias));
protected void setupAlias(String clusterAlias, String indexName, String aliasName) {
+ * Copyright Elasticsearch B.V. and/or licensed to Elasticsearch B.V. under one
+ * or more contributor license agreements. Licensed under the Elastic License
+ * 2.0; you may not use this file except in compliance with the Elastic License
+ * Integration tests for {@code WHERE x IN (subquery)} and {@code WHERE x NOT IN (subquery)}
+ *   <li>SemiJoin (top-level IN), AntiJoin (top-level NOT IN), and MarkJoin (IN/NOT IN under OR)
+ * with the subquery and/or outer FROM referencing remote indices.
+ *   <li>Empty subquery edge cases, nested IN spanning three clusters, and filter-path vs
+ *   <li>Local views as the outer {@code FROM} source with a remote IN subquery, and view bodies
+ * that themselves contain remote IN subqueries.
+ *   <li>Remote-view rejection guard: a view stored on a remote cluster is rejected with
+ * {@code RemoteViewNotSupportedException} even when an IN subquery is present.

+ *

Data layout: each cluster hosts an {@code events} index with 6 docs —
+ * ids 1–6, {@code color} alternates red (odd) / blue (even), {@code tag} set to the cluster
+ * alias ("local", "cluster-a", "remote-b") so rows are attributable in aggregation results.
+        // Local view: red events from the local cluster (ids 1, 3, 5).
+        // Local view whose body contains a remote IN subquery.
+            "FROM events | WHERE id IN (FROM cluster-a:events | WHERE color == \"red\" | KEEP id)"
+        // Remote view stored on cluster-a for the rejection-guard test.
+     * Outer FROM targets both remotes; subquery runs on the local cluster.
+     * The subquery returns the red ids {1,3,5}; each remote has 3 matching rows.
+            | WHERE id IN (FROM events | WHERE color == "red" | KEEP id)
+            // subquery is local-only (1 shard); main plan hits both remotes each with 1 shard
+     * Outer FROM targets the local cluster; subquery runs on remote cluster-a.
+     * The subquery returns red ids {1,3,5}; the local cluster has 3 matching rows.
+            | WHERE id IN (FROM cluster-a:events | WHERE color == "red" | KEEP id)
+            // subquery hits cluster-a (1 shard); main plan is local-only (1 shard)
+     * Both outer FROM and subquery target remote clusters.
+     * Subquery on cluster-a returns red ids {1,3,5}; both remotes contribute 3 matches each.
+            | WHERE id IN (FROM cluster-a:events | WHERE color == "red" | KEEP id)
